title: Egg Chilli Garlic Noodles
tagline: Wok-hot Hakka noodles with scrambled egg, loads of garlic, and chilli oil instead of bottled sauce.
macroNotes: Estimated from ingredients (includes the soya chunks and 3 eggs).
The 100 g soya chunks are doing heavy lifting here — they add ~50 g protein across the two servings for barely any calories. Without them this drops to ~700 kcal and ~28 g protein per serving.
To cut ~150 kcal per serving: use 150 g noodles instead of 200 g and drop to 1.5 tbsp oil total — the chilli oil already brings fat and flavour.

items: 200 g Hakka noodles (or any wheat noodles)
1 tbsp oil + 1 tsp salt for boiling
1 tsp oil for tossing after draining
3 eggs, whisked with a pinch of salt + pepper
100 g dry soya chunks (50 g per serving) — soaked in hot water 10 min, squeezed very dry, chopped small
1.5 tbsp finely chopped garlic (10-12 cloves) — be generous
1 to 1.5 tbsp chilli oil (adjust to tolerance)
1 tsp chilli flakes
1-2 dried red chillies, broken (optional)
1 onion, sliced
1 green chilli, slit
1/2 cup cabbage, shredded
1/2 cup capsicum, thin strips
1/4 tsp Kashmiri chilli powder (colour, mild heat)
1 small pinch turmeric
1/2 tsp crushed coriander seeds OR garam masala (light hand)
1 tsp vinegar or lemon juice
1/2 tsp sugar
2 tbsp oil (neutral — sunflower/rice bran, not olive)
Salt + black pepper to taste
Spring onion greens or coriander to finish
1/2 tbsp butter (optional, for street-style richness)

steps.title: Boil the noodles
steps.body: Boil 200 g noodles in water with 1 tbsp oil + 1 tsp salt until just al dente (firm — they cook more in the wok). Drain immediately, rinse under cold water, and toss with 1 tsp oil. Keep them dry — wet noodles make the whole dish soggy.

steps.title: Prep the soya
steps.body: Soak 100 g dry soya chunks in hot water for 10 minutes, then squeeze them out very dry — this step matters, wet soya waters down the wok and won't take on flavour. Chop them small so they mix through like mince.

steps.title: Scramble the eggs
steps.body: Heat a pan on high with 1 tsp oil. Pour in the 3 whisked eggs and scramble into large soft chunks — don't overcook or break them too fine. Remove and set aside.

steps.title: Build the garlic-chilli base
steps.body: Heat the wok on high until very hot, add 2 tbsp neutral oil. Add 1.5 tbsp chopped garlic and stir till light golden. Add the 1-2 dried red chillies + 1 tsp chilli flakes and toss for 5 seconds — don't burn them.

steps.title: Fry the veg and soya
steps.body: Add the sliced onion + slit green chilli and toss 30 seconds. Add the cabbage + capsicum and stir-fry on high 1 minute so they stay crunchy. Add the squeezed, chopped soya and toss 1 minute so it picks up the garlic and heat.

steps.title: Bloom the spices
steps.body: Lower the flame slightly and add 1/4 tsp Kashmiri chilli powder, a pinch of turmeric, and 1/2 tsp crushed coriander seeds (or garam masala). Stir immediately so they bloom in the oil but don't burn — this is what replaces the bottled sauce.

steps.title: Toss in noodles + chilli oil
steps.body: Add the dry noodles and 1 to 1.5 tbsp chilli oil. Toss hard on high heat using tongs or two ladles so everything coats evenly. Return the scrambled eggs. Season with salt, pepper, and 1/2 tsp sugar, then splash in 1 tsp vinegar or lemon juice for sharpness.

steps.title: Finish
steps.body: Add 1/2 tbsp butter for richness if using, toss in spring onion greens or coriander, give one final toss on high heat and turn off. Serve immediately.

notes: This is the no-bottled-sauce version — chilli oil + Indian spices stand in for chilli garlic sauce and give equal or better flavour. If you do have the sauces, swap in 1.5 tbsp chilli garlic sauce + 1 tbsp soy + 1 tbsp vinegar at the noodle stage.
High heat is everything: preheat the pan 2 minutes before adding oil, and don't overcrowd with veg or you lose the smoky wok flavour.
Add the eggs at the very end so the pieces stay intact.
source: Adapted from my own cooking notes.
